scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every endpoint in AppFollow API v2's published reference (39 operations across General/Users/Reviews/Reviews Stats/Replies/Ratings/App Metadata/Rankings/Featured/ASO Research/ASO Reports/Keywords/Downloads-Revenue) is enumerated below, each covered_by a stream/write or excluded with a specific reason. 7 read streams and 4 pre-existing streams (11 total) plus 11 write actions now cover every practically syncable list/detail resource and every dialect-expressible mutation; the remaining ~17 endpoints are per-request stats/reports/search-tool/admin-adjacent operations excluded for the specific reasons given.

excluded.reason: Bug-tracker-tag update requires a pre-configured external bug tracker integration (Jira/OTRS-style) on the AppFollow account side; the tag value format is tracker-specific (a special vendor prefix per docs) with no generic modeling and no independent read stream to round-trip against — narrower and lower-value than the plain update_review_tags action already covered.

Custom review status is an explicitly-labeled Private Beta feature ("Note: the Review Status feature must be enabled to use this filter" on the reviews GET endpoint too) not generally available on all accounts; excluded until the feature is GA.

Report-a-concern flags a review to the app store for policy review (App Store/Google Play content-moderation escalation) — an infrequent, high-consequence moderation action rather than an ordinary reverse-ETL write; its irreversible store-side effect and the absence of any corresponding read-back stream make it a poor fit for this migration pass.
Bulk custom-source review upload accepts a multipart file payload (a review export file), not a JSON record body the write dialect's body_type json/form/none shapes can express.

Featured reviews is a filtered subset of the same review records already covered by the reviews stream (same fields, editorially curated selection) — covering it as a second stream would duplicate reviews' record shape for no additional data.
Returns a generated natural-language AI summary paragraph, not tabular record data suited to a warehouse destination; no stable primary key or schema across calls.

Semantic/sentiment tag analysis is an Advanced-API custom add-on (100 credits/request, the most expensive operation in the catalog) layered on top of the already-covered reviews stream's own data — narrow, account-tier-gated analytics rather than a core sync resource.
Aggregate review-count statistics derivable from the reviews/reviews_summary streams already covered; no fields beyond what those two streams already expose per date/app.
Per-version review stats are a narrower aggregation of the same reviews stream data (grouped by app_version, already present on every reviews record) already covered.
Rating-distribution stats duplicate the star-bucket fields (stars1..stars5) already exposed by the covered reviews_summary/ratings/ratings_history streams.

Team-productivity metric (reply counts per user/date) about the AppFollow account's own reply activity, not app/review/rating domain data; a narrow operational-reporting endpoint with a per-day additional price tier.
Same team-productivity category as replies/count (average reply turnaround time per user) — operational reporting on the account's own support team, not app-store domain data.
Same team-productivity category as replies/count and replies/speed — reply-rate statistics about the account's own reply activity.

Google Play Console rating breakdown is a store-specific variant of the already-covered ratings_history stream's data (same star-bucket/date shape restricted to one store and one additional-priced tier), not independently new data.

Public top-charts snapshot is store-wide reference data (a category/country/device leaderboard) independent of any tracked app in the caller's AppFollow account — a lookup/reference tool, not account-scoped syncable data with a natural per-app fan-out key.
Ad-hoc keyword/app search tool (arbitrary search term in, ranked app list out) — a query-time lookup utility, not a stable syncable resource tied to a tracked app or collection.

ASO keyword-suggestion research tool (given a seed term, returns related keyword ideas) — an exploratory research utility with no natural primary key tied to the caller's tracked apps, same category as aso/search.

App Store "Featured" placement history is a narrow editorial-placement report with its own additional 30-day price tier; out of scope for this pass alongside the other *_reports/aso research endpoints.
Apple Search Ads keyword-recommendation tool, a paid-acquisition research utility rather than syncable app/review/rating domain data.
General ASO report aggregates data already covered by the keywords/rankings streams (position/visibility over time) into a summarized report shape.
Apple Search Ads campaign performance report — paid-acquisition advertising data, not organic app/review/rating/keyword domain data this connector otherwise covers.
Google Discovery Campaign (GDC) advertising report — same paid-acquisition category as reports/aso/search_ads, not organic ASO domain data.
